free_text: A study of the 9Be(p,a)6Li reaction from 3.5 to
Author's tabular
data, published on pp.266-267, 275, 277 of this article
Part of
data is given.
Silicon p-type diffused layer counters with
resistivities ranging from 50 to 300 omega x cm.
The elastic and inelastic scattering of protons and
alpha particles were measured with 10000 omega x cm
diffused-layer and lithium-drifted detectors.
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year
SD: ref. on ND/A corrected and moved to
REL-REF. BIB updated. Columns in Subents 002, 011.
swapped according to presentation at the tables.
Titles of digitizing errors changed according to
Dict. SF9=EXP in REACTION code in Subents 002-016
deleted.
Changes in all subents.
Metallic beryllium targets with areal densities from
50-100 mu-g/cm**2 were made by vacuum evaporation on
formvar and self-supporting beryllium foils of about
500 mu-g/cm2 were made by vacuum evaporation and were
used to determine absolute cross sections for reactions
The absolute cross-section
scale has been determined by measuring the elastic
scattering of protons from a thin beryllium foil whose
thickness was determined by weighing it on a
micro-balance.
Corrections were made for carbon and oxygen
impurities in the target
The uncertainty in absolute cross section
Relative error
Tabular data obtained from fig.3-4 of

Result was taken from Nucl.Data A(1966)1,203.

6Li targets of about 50 mu-g/cm2, made by vacuum
evaporation of isotopically enriched 6Li (99.96%) onto
formvar.
Three separate targets (LiF, natural lithium, enriched
6Li) were used for reactions 6Li+alpha.
the absolute cr.sect. were
determined by normalization to the diff. cr.sect.
measurements for the 19F(p,alpha) reaction obtained
with the gas target.
Error from graphic bars (statistical)
Uncertainty in absolute cross section.
Since the performed normalization involved three
separate targets (LiF, natural lithium and enriched
Li6) the absolute values obtained for the cross
section suffer from rather large uncertainties.
Relative error
